note: This record does not modify, replace, or supersede any file in PR #79. It is an additive approval overlay referencing the exact candidate by SHA.

priorRejectedCandidate.note: PR #78 (v3.1) is preserved unmodified as historical rejected-candidate evidence. This approval record does not touch, close, or alter PR #78 in any way.

userApprovalEvidence.verdictTranslationNote: Informal Thai for 'okay, that's fine / good to go' -- recorded verbatim as given, with a plain-language gloss for non-Thai-reading reviewers.
verdictContext: Given after Coco delivered the Archer Attack v3.2 package in status READY_FOR_HUMAN_REVIEW (PR #79).
User confirmed the visual result looked acceptable.
The benchmark direction discussed alongside the verdict emphasized 'looks smooth at normal speed' as the operative standard.
The user did not approve, and this record does not interpret the verdict as approving, a rule that every animation must use 12 frames.

approved: the exact visual package v3.2 as delivered in PR #79
the final 12-frame sequence specific to this package
the motion-smoothing quality of v3.2 (frames 004 and 006 specifically)
identity, anatomy, and bow/arrow/string continuity of this package
the exact hashes and timing recorded in PR #79 and reproduced in this record
notApproved: a rule that every animation must have 12 frames
a rule that every animation must use the same frame count
a rule that every action must add exactly 2 in-between frames
a rule that every animation must use Archer's specific timing values

immutablePackageBoundary.description: This approval is scoped exactly to the 12 frame binaries listed above, identified by SHA-256, as they exist at PR #79 commit ddd0b655f867d0b01106a9e1d274234e1e9b71e9. Any future change to any of those 12 files -- including format normalization -- invalidates this approval record for the changed file(s) and requires a new approval cycle.

knownFormatObservation.finding: Frame 004 is PNG RGBA, 16-bit per channel. Every other frame in this package -- and every frame across the entire approved Archer pipeline (Neutral Master, Idle, Move, Attack v2/v3/v3.1) -- is 8-bit RGBA.
knownFormatObservation.decodeDisplayReviewValidatorStatus: PASS -- decoded and rendered correctly throughout review; PR #79's validator (extended with 16-bit-aware decoding) confirms alpha bounds are correct.
knownFormatObservation.actionTakenInThisApprovalTask: none -- binary not re-encoded, resized, or otherwise modified
knownFormatObservation.followUpDescription: Before any Runtime Integration of this or a related package, the target asset decoder/toolchain must be verified to correctly support 16-bit-per-channel RGBA PNG. This observation does not invalidate the exact-visual approval recorded here, but it does gate runtime promotion independently of this approval.

gitDiffCheckObservation.finding: git diff --check between PR #79's parent commit and PR #79 head, scoped to this file, reports 'new blank line at EOF' and exits 2.
gitDiffCheckObservation.reproductionResult: exit 2, 'docs/reviews/archer-attack-v3-2-technical-qa-report.md:52: new blank line at EOF.'
gitDiffCheckObservation.verificationMethod: SHA-256 and byte-for-byte cmp against the file as originally extracted from the approved source ZIP -- confirmed identical (not drift, not modified after import)
gitDiffCheckObservation.classification: whitespace-lint-observation, not binary drift, not a visual defect
gitDiffCheckObservation.actionTakenInThisApprovalTask: none -- file not modified, because doing so would change the exact-approved PR #79 candidate snapshot
gitDiffCheckObservation.note: PR #79's own description states 'git diff --check: clean'. That statement is not accurate for the full history of the file's introduction and is corrected here for the record. It is NOT accurate to say PR #79's git diff --check is clean when checked against its parent commit for this file. This does not change any approval conclusion -- the file content is confirmed unmodified and correct.

standardCoversQualityOf: looks smooth at normal playback speed
true motion progression (not a mesh-warp/scale-collapse substitute for motion)
frame spacing appropriate to the motion being depicted
character identity consistency across all frames
anatomy continuity (hands, limbs, no missing/extra parts)
weapon/prop continuity (for Archer specifically: bow/arrow/string continuity)
timing and release-beat preservation where a release/impact event exists
technical QA (format, dimensions, alpha bounds) and comparative review against the prior candidate
standardDoesNotMandate: every animation must have exactly 12 frames
every character/animation must use the same frame count as another
every action must add exactly 2 in-between frames
every animation must reuse Archer's specific per-frame timing values
frames must be added even when the existing animation already reads as smooth
animationQualityBenchmarkV1.guidingPrinciple: Use as many frames as are actually needed for the motion to read as smooth while preserving the weight/impact of the action -- no more, no fewer. Frame count and timing are motion-driven decisions per animation, not a fixed template.
animationQualityBenchmarkV1.benchmarkActivationScope: This authorization activates the benchmark as a quality standard for future Archer (and by extension, other character) animation review. It does not itself approve any other character's existing or future animation -- each candidate still requires its own exact-package approval record.

runtimeRestrictions.note: This record authorizes exact-package visual/quality approval only. It does not authorize merging PR #79, runtime integration, canonical promotion, or any Core Logic/Combat/projectile/marker/Game Loop change.

nextGateNote: This gate is not started by this record. It must independently verify that the target asset decoder/toolchain correctly supports 16-bit-per-channel RGBA PNG (specifically for frame 004) before any Runtime Integration work begins.
